title: Pandiaka lanata
taxonomy: Pandiaka lanata is classified as a species within the family Amaranthaceae. This classification places it within the order Caryophyllales. The family Amaranthaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the amaranth or pigweed family. Pandiaka lanata is a part of this diverse family, which comprises over 175 genera and 2,000 species. Within its classification, Pandiaka lanata is a member of the genus Pandiaka.

intro: Pandiaka lanata, also known as African tea or wild tea, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Amaranthaceae family. The health benefits of Pandiaka lanata are attributed to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in reducing fever, treating diarrhea, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pandiaka lanata is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water to relieve various ailments.
